reviews.comment:
Luego de hacer la fila para ingresar al surtidor de nafta, y en el momento que nos tocaba cargar a nosotros, nos colocan el cono cerrando la playa pues había llegado el camión con combustible. Una Sra que estaba atrás nuestro (auto blanco de la foto) le realizó señas al playero, quien le respondió con ademanes que espere y con la cabeza le dijo que si. La hizo ingresar de contramano por la playa, sorteando los conos y le despachó nafta. Al realizar nosotros el reclamo, nos respondieron que le habían cargado nafta porque era empleada del ACA. Mi respuesta fue que si la ley es pareja, tiene que serlo para todos, no porque sea empleada de la empresa, la Sra tiene que tener "coronita". Lamentable situación vivida que nos incomodó a todos. Tanto mi marido como yo somos socios del ACA, estamos pensando en borrarnos.

(Translated by Google)
After waiting in line for the gas pump, just as it was our turn, they put up a cone to close off the area because the fuel truck had arrived. A woman behind us (in the white car in the photo) signaled to the attendant, who gestured for her to wait and nodded. He directed her to drive the wrong way down the pump, around the cones, and filled her tank. When we complained, they told us she'd been given preferential treatment because she was an ACA employee. My response was that if the law is supposed to be the same for everyone, it shouldn't apply to everyone, not just because she's an employee. It was a regrettable situation that made us all uncomfortable. Both my husband and I are ACA members, and we're considering canceling our memberships.
reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/2511053987051317301/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURCLXNUeE5nEAE
